title: Centropogon glabratus
taxonomy: Centropogon glabratus is classified under the family Melastomataceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the melastome family. This family is part of the order Myrtales, which also includes other related plant families such as the myrtle family and the heath family. The order Myrtales is part of the clade Rosidae, a large group of flowering plants that also includes families like the rose family and the apple family. The clade Rosidae is a part of the larger clade Asterids, which contains many diverse flowering plant families. Further classification of Centropogon glabratus can be found within its genus and species classification within the family Melastomataceae.

It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America and has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Some studies suggest that extracts from the plant may possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. intro: Centropogon glabratus, commonly known as Brazilian peppertree or Brazilian fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. Research suggests that Centropogon glabratus has potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its use in traditional medicine to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Centropogon glabratus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Centropogon glabratus is traditionally used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures to treat various health conditions, and is also used in the production of dietary supplements.
